chapter_title: Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
text:
(a) Subtitles A, B, and D and Chapter 551A (Off-highway Vehicles) do not apply to the operation of an electric bicycle.

(b) A person may not operate an electric bicycle unless the electric motor disengages or ceases to function either:

(1) when the operator stops pedaling; or

(2) when the brakes are applied.

(c) A person may not operate a Class 3 electric bicycle, as defined by Section 664.001 (Definitions) , unless the person is at least 15 years of age. This subsection does not prohibit a person who is under 15 years of age from riding on a Class 3 bicycle as a passenger.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 551. Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
##### § 551.107 — Operation of Electric Bicycle

(a) Subtitles A, B, and D and Chapter 551A (Off-highway Vehicles) do not apply to the operation of an electric bicycle.

(b) A person may not operate an electric bicycle unless the electric motor disengages or ceases to function either:

    (1) when the operator stops pedaling; or

    (2) when the brakes are applied.

(c) A person may not operate a Class 3 electric bicycle, as defined by Section 664.001 (Definitions) , unless the person is at least 15 years of age. This subsection does not prohibit a person who is under 15 years of age from riding on a Class 3 bicycle as a passenger.

*Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 915 (H.B.3607), Sec. 20.007, eff. September 1, 2021.*
